label: Kidney etc.
quick_take: Kidney cancer is almost universally rising — but the rise is split between *detection* (incidental CT findings of small renal masses) and *real burden* (obesity, hypertension, smoking cohorts). Indigenous populations and a Naples-sized cluster of Italian men are carrying the highest current rates.

findings.title: Canada NWT & Yukon women, vol IV: ASR 23.8 — 5.6× the world female median
findings.text: Alaska n=89→249, Montana n=218→409, Oklahoma n=893→1770. The Indigenous excess is unusually prominent for this site and spans generations. Yukon vol IV sits on single-digit counts + a pop_warning (vol VII); the Alaska / Montana / Oklahoma aggregate numerators carry the statistical weight. Coding_break: kidney definition shifted between ICD-7 and later volumes, so vol IV → XII contrasts inherit that caveat.
findings.image_alt: CI5 trend chart — Canada NWT & Yukon women, vol IV: ASR 23.8 — 5.6× the world female median

findings.title: Japanese and US-Asian catch-ups: huge
findings.text: Miyagi men 1.13 → 12.46 (+1003 %); Osaka men 2.92 → 11.76 (U-shape); LA Filipino men 1.11 → 9.98 (+799 %); Singapore Chinese men 1.32 → 10.44 (+691 %). Much of this is incidental CT detection; some is real.
findings.image_alt: CI5 trend chart — Japanese and US-Asian catch-ups: huge

findings.title: Shanghai men: classic U-shape
findings.image_alt: CI5 trend chart — Shanghai men: classic U-shape

findings.title: Spain Tarragona and Israel-Arab women: U-shape recoveries
findings.text: 1.54 → 1.32 → 4.82 and 1.26 → 0.79 → 3.40. Consistent with a late-arriving CT-detection wave.
findings.image_alt: CI5 trend chart — Spain Tarragona and Israel-Arab women: U-shape recoveries

findings.title: Morocco Casablanca women and India Chennai women show bimodal age curves
findings.text: with a secondary peak at age band 14 (65–69). The shapes are unusual for kidney cancer and deserve a sanity-check — could be Wilms-tumour vs renal-cell confusion in older volumes.
findings.image_alt: CI5 trend chart — Morocco Casablanca women and India Chennai women show bimodal age curves

findings.title: Women-heavy registries
findings.text: The Gambia 0.13 (M 0.14, F 1.04 — nearly 1:8 ratio, an exceptional inversion!), Barshi/Paranda 0.27, Northwest Territories + Yukon 0.36. Kidney cancer is usually 2:1 male:female; the Gambia's reversal is notable — but numerators are 3–6 cases per cell and NWT/Yukon numbers show ASR/crude divergence (population ageing). Treat as indicative, not quantitative.

findings.title: Ethnic contrast inside USA is 11–21× depending on volume
findings.text: US White-to-Asian ratios were 21× in vol IV, around 10× in vol X — narrowing but still large.
findings.image_alt: CI5 trend chart — Ethnic contrast inside USA is 11–21× depending on volume

findings.title: cancer_warning
findings.text: ICD-7 was used for the first three volumes, with some discrepancies versus later volumes. Don't read too much into the vol I–II → vol IV step.
Incidental-imaging detection (late-1980s onward) accounts for a substantial part of the Western rise; corrected-for-stage analyses in national data would separate real from apparent.
The Indigenous (Alaska, Canada NWT, Montana, Oklahoma) women's excess needs a focused review — the magnitude is striking and persistent.
A "kidney + bladder as a single urinary-tract story in urbanising East Asia" paper with Shanghai, Miyagi, Osaka, Singapore, Seoul would land well.
The bimodal Moroccan and Indian kidney age-curves could reveal a coding issue with Wilms tumour at the adult boundary.
